findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "analyze_bill" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet:
Read a bill from the user's description and create it for payment. This is the first step. Capture every address ahave asked.
    If analyze_bill reported a need for ext

evidence.name: get_new_auth_token
evidence.description:
Get a fresh auth token when another tool fails with an authorization error. Pass
    the user's current refresh_token; returns a new auth_token/refresh_token pair to use
    on the retried call and on later calls. Call this only in response to an auth failure,
    not preemptively.

    Args:

evidence.name: fetch_bill_status
evidence.description:
Check the payment status of a submitted bill. Read `value` for the current status.
    statusEvaluatedDescription is only present when the payment was declined or failed,
    and then explains why - relay that reason to the user. It is omitted for every other
    status, so treat its presence as a d

evidence.name: request_bill_cancellation
evidence.description:
Cancel a submitted bill so it will not be paid. Use when the user wants to stop a
    bill they submitted; cancellation may be rejected if the payment has already
    progressed too far - relay any error to the user. Not for correcting bill details:
    re-run analyze_bill for that.

    Args:

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "analyze_bill" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Read a bill from the user's description and create it for payment. This is the first step. Capture every address and identifier on the bill (remit-to address, a…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "analyze_bill" stacks urgency/override words (important, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_new_auth_token" references sensitive artifacts (auth token, authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: analyze_bill · Private data: analyze_bill, confirm_payment_intent, get_new_auth_token, fetch_bill_status, request_bill_cancellation · Public sink: confirm_payment_intent · Destructive: request_bill_cancellation.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: analyze_bill · Private data: analyze_bill, confirm_payment_intent, get_new_auth_token, fetch_bill_status, request_bill_cancellation · Public sink: confirm_payment_intent · Destructive: request_bill_cancellation

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
